mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a walk-forward t-test over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which sustains corporate earnings momentum and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in quarterly regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side bottlenecks, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances during late-cycle expansions, or the signal loses predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 3.5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase above 0.4% signals accelerating inflation that forces equity valuation compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered months, or the inflation threshold shows zero incremental predictive power over a baseline model in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months following payroll growth, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing quarterly GDP growth and declining retail sales indicates a demand shock that compresses profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth < 1.5% and RSAFS month-over-month change < 0%
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in quarterly panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above 0.3% reflects strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which supports near-term equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered months during recovery phases, or the signal shows no statistical edge (p > 0.10) in a walk-forward analysis over 3 years.
